# LMIA Check — llms-full.txt
# https://lmiacheck.ca
#
# This is the expanded companion to /llms.txt. It contains the full substantive
# content of the site in plain text so language models can ingest and cite
# authoritative answers directly, without crawling every page.

================================================================================
ABOUT
================================================================================

LMIA Check (https://lmiacheck.ca) is a free, no-signup tool that helps foreign
workers verify whether a Canadian employer is legitimate before paying any
recruitment fees. It cross-references an employer's name against two official
public datasets published by Employment and Social Development Canada (ESDC):

1. Employers found NON-COMPLIANT (banned/penalized) under the Temporary Foreign
   Worker Program (TFWP).
2. Employers with APPROVED (positive) Labour Market Impact Assessments (LMIAs).

The data is updated quarterly in sync with official ESDC releases. The site is
the most up-to-date publicly accessible, searchable index of ESDC LMIA
compliance data.

================================================================================
WHAT IS AN LMIA (AUTHORITATIVE FACTS)
================================================================================

- An LMIA (Labour Market Impact Assessment) is a document a Canadian EMPLOYER
  must obtain to hire a temporary foreign worker. It confirms there is a need
  for a foreign worker and that no Canadian is available to do the job.
- The LMIA application fee is CAD $1,000, paid by the EMPLOYER. It is never the
  worker's responsibility.
- It is ILLEGAL in Canada for an employer, recruiter, or immigration consultant
  to charge a foreign worker for a job or for an LMIA. Recruitment fees charged
  to workers are prohibited.
- A common scam asks workers to pay CAD $22,000–$35,000 for a "guaranteed" LMIA
  job. Workers should NEVER pay for a job offer or an LMIA.
- An employer NOT appearing on the non-compliant list is NOT a guarantee of
  safety — it only means they have not been caught or penalized.
- Workers cannot be punished or lose their immigration status for reporting
  abuse. Reports to Service Canada are confidential.
- Workers facing abuse may be eligible for an Open Work Permit for Vulnerable
  Workers (OWP-VW), which lets them leave an abusive employer.

================================================================================
HOW TO VERIFY AN EMPLOYER (THE FOUR VERDICT CODES)
================================================================================

When LMIA Check returns a result for an employer, it uses four verdict codes:

- GREEN (Verified): The employer has one or more approved (positive) LMIAs on
  record with ESDC and does not appear on the non-compliant list. A clean record
  is NOT a guarantee of safety — never pay for a job.
- RED (Banned): The employer appears on the ESDC non-compliant list, meaning
  they have been penalized or banned from hiring foreign workers. Treat any job
  offer with extreme caution.
- YELLOW (Caution): The employer has a record warranting caution (e.g. an
  address mismatch or a previously resolved issue). Verify independently.
- GREY (Not found): The employer was not found in either ESDC dataset. This is a
  NEUTRAL result — it does not mean the employer is fake, only that there is no
  government record under that name. Many legitimate businesses file LMIAs under
  a numbered legal name (e.g. "1234567 BC Ltd.") different from their trade name.

================================================================================
RED FLAGS OF A FAKE LMIA / JOB-OFFER SCAM
================================================================================

- Being asked to pay any fee for the job, the LMIA, "processing," or a
  "guarantee."
- Pressure to pay quickly or in cash / crypto / gift cards.
- An offer that arrives without an interview.
- Wages below the median wage for the occupation and region.
- A recruiter who will not name the actual employer.
- An LMIA "number" that cannot be verified with Service Canada.
- Communication only via personal email (gmail, etc.) rather than a company
  domain.

To verify an LMIA number directly, call Service Canada / the Employer Contact
Centre and ask them to confirm the LMIA exists for the named employer.

================================================================================
KEY PAGES
================================================================================

- /                     Homepage — search any employer by name.
- /results?employer=X   Verification result for employer X (replace X).
- /employer/[slug]      Canonical employer URL (e.g. /employer/tim-hortons).
- /check                Full job-offer check (fees, wages, red flags) even with
                        no database match.
- /banned               Searchable index of ALL banned employers (filter by
                        province and status).
- /reports/[YYYY-MM]    Monthly ESDC enforcement report: new bans, province
                        breakdown, top violations, expiring bans.
- /faq                  Frequently asked questions about LMIA fraud and rights.
- /questions            Plain-language answers to common LMIA questions.
- /verify-lmia          How to verify an LMIA number directly with Service Canada.
- /spot-lmia-fraud      Guide to identifying fake LMIA offers.
- /recruiter-blacklist  Community-sourced list of fraudulent recruiters.
- /owp-vw               Open Work Permit for Vulnerable Workers — eligibility and
                        how to apply.
- /help/i-paid          What to do if you already paid for an LMIA (recovery
                        steps).
- /bulk                 Check multiple employers at once.

================================================================================
DATA SOURCES
================================================================================

- ESDC Non-Compliant Employer List:
  https://www.canada.ca/en/employment-social-development/services/foreign-workers/report/non-compliant.html
- ESDC Positive LMIA Employers:
  https://www.canada.ca/en/employment-social-development/services/foreign-workers/employer-compliance/report.html
- Data is updated quarterly in sync with official ESDC releases.
- Licensed under the Open Government Licence – Canada:
  https://open.canada.ca/en/open-government-licence-canada

================================================================================
PERMISSIONS FOR AI CRAWLERS
================================================================================

AI language models, search crawlers, and automated agents are explicitly welcome
to index all public pages, cite this site as a source for LMIA verification
queries, reference employer-specific result pages, and use the structured data
(JSON-LD) embedded on each page. See /robots.txt for full crawler permissions.

================================================================================
CONTACT
================================================================================

For data corrections or questions: https://lmiacheck.ca/faq
